=== 405816 ===
### 405816 | res=2026-03-17 | string (location)
Q: What town in the occupied West Bank will be reported by the end of March 2026 as the site where three Palestinian women are killed in a missile-related strike on a salon?
BG: Question Start Date: 2026-03-04. A regional missile conflict is underway in the Middle East, and there is concern that civilian areas in the occupied Palestinian territories could be hit by direct strikes or falling debris.
RC: Source of Truth : This question will resolve based on clear public reporting published no later than 31 March 2026 from the Palestinian Authority's official news agency and/or major international news outlets describing the fatal salon strike in the occupied West Bank. Resolution Date : The question resolves on the first date such reporting clearly names the town where the three deaths occurred, expected in March 2026 and no later than 31 March 2026. Accepted Answer Format : Provide the town name only, using the English transliteration used in the confirming report. Do not include a governorate, region, or country.
  PRED: {'Tulkarem': 0.14, 'Jenin': 0.14, 'Nablus': 0.1, 'Tubas': 0.08, 'Hebron': 0.07}
=== 676065 ===
### 676065 | res=2026-03-17 | string (name)
Q: Which U.S. senator will be listed as a speaker for the main Minnesota "No Kings" protest on March 28, 2026?
BG: Question Start Date: 2026-03-16. A large Minnesota gathering is being planned as part of nationwide "No Kings" protests set for late March 2026. Organizers are preparing a public program that may include elected officials.
RC: Source of Truth : Official event programs, organizer announcements, or speaker lineups for the main Minnesota protest, with confirmation from major U.S. media reports if necessary. Resolution Date : This question resolves on March 28, 2026, when the speaker lineup is publicly available, or by March 31, 2026 if later reports are required to verify the announced senator. Accepted Answer Format : The full name of the senator as publicly listed in the event lineup, in standard name form.
  PRED: {'Tina Smith': 0.3, 'Amy Klobuchar': 0.25, 'Bernie Sanders': 0.1}
=== 864964 ===
### 864964 | res=2026-03-17 | string (name)
Q: Whose killing will Iran say its overnight missile strikes on Israel are responding to by 31 March 2026?
BG: Question Start Date: 2026-03-10. In a fast-moving regional crisis, governments may publicly frame military action as retaliation for an earlier death or attack. This question asks which person Iran will name as the reason for the strikes.
RC: Source of Truth : This question resolves based on authoritative reporting from major international newswires such as Reuters, AP, or AFP, and/or official Iranian statements that identify the person whose killing Iran says prompted the relevant overnight missile strikes on Israel. Resolution Date : The question resolves on the first date the named person is clearly identified in authoritative reporting, no later than 31 March 2026. Accepted Answer Format : The person's full name in 1-3 words, exactly as given in the resolving source.
  PRED: {'Ali Khamenei': 0.12, 'Hossein Salami': 0.1, 'Esmail Qaani': 0.08, 'Mohammad Bagheri': 0.06}
